name: Baseline-018: scaling-residual detector (the weird-bet falsification — dual-LM signal is WEAKER than single-LM)
description: Score = mean-per-token-logp(Pythia-70M) − mean-per-token-logp(Pythia-410M). Uses the inverse-scaling FINDING as a feature: the gradient between two LMs of different sizes isolates the 'small-LM-specific surprise' signal that traces the scaling curve. Genuinely novel signal axis.
pre_stated_prediction: submissions/baseline_018_scaling_residual/PRE_STATED_PREDICTION.md committed at commit 60b9c5e BEFORE the gauntlet run. Predicted distribution: ~22% PASS (highest of any baseline), ~25% 3/4 (modal), ~50% partial/fail. Actual outcome (2/4) is in the 25% '2/4' band — modal shape correct, but the specific D2 AUC and length-delta predictions overshot.

reported.note: 2/4. D1, D2 AUC pass; D3, D4 fail. The dual-LM difference signal is WEAKER than either single-LM signal: compared to Pythia-70M (B-016, 3/4), D1 dropped 0.816 -> 0.726, D2 dropped 0.907 -> 0.733, D2-length-delta dropped +0.103 -> -0.071. The composite signal amplifies per-record noise faster than it sharpens discrimination. The 'use the scaling curve as a feature' hypothesis is falsified empirically: while the means clearly differ between 70M and 410M (matching the scaling-curve FINDING), the per-record difference variance dominates the discriminative gap.

prediction_calibration.direction_calibration: CONFIRMED for eighth consecutive baseline (misc -0.71 > truth -2.09)
prediction_calibration.honest_note: Twelfth in-session falsification. The 'novel weird signal will surprise us' hypothesis loses: the dual-LM gradient signal is weaker than the single-LM signal at either endpoint. The empirical lesson: composite features from per-record noisy estimates can amplify noise more than signal. Pre-registration was load-bearing here too — without it, I might have framed Baseline-018 as 'still better than random' rather than 'specifically falsified vs the single-LM baselines that brackeded it.' Direction confirmed for eighth consecutive baseline; direction-of-effect is now the most stable signal in this domain by a wide margin.

notes: The weird-bet falsification suggests that LM-typicality detection has a method-class ceiling around Pythia-70M's 3/4 result. Adding orthogonal LM features doesn't help. The next disciplined paths are: (1) genuinely orthogonal feature axes (entity-density, knowledge-graph lookup, cross-vendor disagreement); (2) an ensemble that combines the strongest single-LM signal (Pythia-70M) with a complementary feature targeted at D1 specifically (factual-error / pseudoscience discrimination); (3) a methodology paper documenting that LM-typicality alone cannot break the dark-core floor on D1 axis. The seven-method floor stands. The 12 in-session falsifications now include this empirically-tested innovative-signal hypothesis.
